115061.explanation: We prefer to participate through consultation and dialogue, for example during our accessible VVD Cafés. Referendums are not a standard solution. We gladly provide customized solutions.

115063.explanation: We want to give entrepreneurs space, with customization per location. Meanwhile, we keep an eye on our green environment in Halderberge.

115068.explanation: This really won’t make a difference in the sustainability challenge. Regarding air quality, Halderberge suffers more from fine dust from the ports of Rotterdam and Antwerp settling in West Brabant and emissions from agriculture and transport sectors.

115070.explanation: We see this as customization per neighborhood, but not as a direct government task. If initiatives come from society, we should cooperate but not pay for or organize everything ourselves.

115071.explanation: We want to start a trial with twice-yearly free bulky waste collection at home. A trip to the recycling center still poses a barrier for many. This way we want to prevent illegal dumping.

115073.explanation: We ensure a well-accessible public space, also for people with disabilities. Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for making their own buildings accessible.

115074.explanation: We want to give entrepreneurs more space to build homes also in the countryside, while paying attention to the green environment in Halderberge. The municipality remains in charge of where it is allowed and assesses each plan.

115075.explanation: For people with social assistance benefits, special social assistance is already available to keep activities like sports and culture accessible. Additionally, many social initiatives enable sports and culture.

115076.explanation: We want to use community funds efficiently and not automatically grant extra subsidies.

115080.explanation: In Halderberge, we already have seven BOAs (special enforcement officers). Yet there remains a need for a strong force that has the time to specialize more. For example, in rural areas or in preventing through traffic by trucks. If an expansion is needed for that, we are open to it.

115081.explanation: The targeted use of camera surveillance not only helps track down offenders better, but also works preventively, for example against illegal dumping at environmental parks.

115082.explanation: The most important thing is that such a facility is accessible to our own residents. That is currently the case, and often it is better if it is a bit outside our municipality. Therefore, we do not see this as a necessity.

115085.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes undermines the recreational character of holiday parks. Moreover, we see that this is being abused by housing large numbers of labor migrants in holiday parks. We prefer to focus on affordable home ownership.

115086.explanation: We want to build 2,000 additional homes in all villages. We are also looking at expansion beyond existing village boundaries.

115088.explanation: We want to remain one of the cheapest municipalities to live in the Netherlands. Waiving taxes in one place means higher charges elsewhere. We do not want a waterbed effect but generally lower municipal taxes.

115089.explanation: Working is the best form of participation. We want to encourage residents to progress towards paid work. This is easier if the 'poverty trap' between non-workers and workers is reduced or prevented.

115091.explanation: Halderberge has insufficient charging points. People with electric cars must be able to park close to home. We want to keep low charging rates leading.

115093.explanation: The quality of care is leading, not someone’s belief system. We will not contribute to polarization. That happens when you only associate with your own group.

115094.explanation: The quality of care must be leading. Costs in youth care are spiraling out of control, mainly due to too high an influx. This must change. Therefore, we focus strongly on prevention.

115095.explanation: We want to remain one of the cheapest municipalities to live in the Netherlands. When there are budget surpluses, we want to reduce the burden on our residents. We only agree to an increase in property tax if our residents truly see something in return.

115097.explanation: There is insufficient space for our entrepreneurs. Therefore, where appropriate, we want to expand our industrial areas, giving local entrepreneurs priority when allocating business premises.

115098.explanation: We want the priority position for social rental housing to apply to more groups. We focus on ensuring that our own residents get priority.

115103.explanation: The government has decided that mandatory school swimming will not return. It costs too much money and teachers lose too much time on it. Therefore, we do not find it logical that the municipality should finance school swimming. We prefer the government to take responsibility.

115107.explanation: In Halderberge, you can currently usually only build up to 3 or 4 stories high. Given today’s housing shortage, that is simply too limited. We say: where possible, we dare to build higher – up to 6 or 7 stories! This makes homes much more affordable.

115108.explanation: In practice, we see this is often abused, for example by housing labor migrants in poor conditions in small rooms. We do not want to encourage this.

115109.explanation: Everyone should be able to park their car near their home. This also applies to people living in the center. People doing shopping must also be able to park well without causing inconvenience to residents of our village centers.

115110.explanation: In 2025-2026, we made the center of Oudenbosch low-traffic, reducing traffic but still allowing shoppers, people with mobility issues, and suppliers to reach the shops. We want to wait and see the effects.

115111.explanation: We want to focus on more affordable owner-occupied, mid-rent, and transitional housing. This gives first-time buyers a fair chance and lets seniors move on to suitable homes.

115112.explanation: Halderberge already hosts an above-average number of Ukrainians (over 200), leads in housing status holders, and has an above-average number of labor migrants. Therefore, we will respond negatively to questions or requests from initiators of refugee housing.

115113.explanation: Residents still experience BOAs too little as visible and approachable enforcers in the neighborhood. The VVD wants to make them more visible, so residents see and notice enforcement, not just read that it happens. This can be done with bodycams.

115061.explanation: We stand for open, honest, and transparent decision-making. Residents should be able to think along and sometimes even decide. A referendum can help with major choices. This way, decisions better fit what people want. We will also continue active dialogue with residents.

115063.explanation: We give space to farmers for new ideas. But it must fit with the environment, traffic, and nature. We assess each situation to determine what is good and safe. “Yes, provided that” is our starting point.

115068.explanation: Use is allowed, but we pay attention to health and nuisance. Good information and rules remain necessary. We want clean air in the villages. We should not unnecessarily bother each other and must use common sense.

115070.explanation: A central show is indeed festive, safer, and causes less litter. It brings people together and reduces neighborhood nuisance. But we do not see this as a municipal task. If residents want to take this initiative, the municipality should certainly support and facilitate it.

115071.explanation: We want to make it easy to neatly dispose of bulky waste. Sometimes free is possible, for example for people who cannot manage themselves. But it must also remain affordable for the municipality. We seek a fair solution.

115073.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Accessible buildings are part of that. We help entrepreneurs with advice and support. This way we make our villages more inclusive.

115074.explanation: We prefer not to build in rural areas. Only if it really serves a social purpose and under strict conditions. This way we protect landscapes and nature. Most housing should be in and near villages.

115075.explanation: Sport is healthy and provides social contact. Even people with little money must be able to participate. Therefore, we want to remove barriers. This keeps everyone moving.

115076.explanation: Local broadcasters inform and connect. Extra funding is possible, but we first look at usefulness and necessity. We want the money to be well spent. Clear agreements are part of that.

115080.explanation: Visibility on the street works. BOAs, together with the police, quickly tackle nuisance. Reports receive swift responses. This gives a feeling of safety. BOAs outside, administrative staff inside.

115081.explanation: Cameras help prevent and solve incidents. We use them targeted at risk locations. Privacy rules remain in effect, of course. Safety and carefulness go hand in hand.

115082.explanation: We want good and safe shelter. Sometimes regional cooperation is smarter and faster. We look at what works best for victims. It is about protection and immediate help.

115085.explanation: We invest in more tourism. Permanent residence in holiday homes does not fit with that.

115086.explanation: We prefer to build within villages and the designated building concentrations. This protects the landscape. If it serves a social purpose, tailored solutions are conceivable for us.

115088.explanation: People with little money sometimes really need support. Tax waivers prevent additional debts. We do not let vulnerable people fall through the cracks. That is social and sensible.

115089.explanation: More and more people struggle to make ends meet. With a higher threshold, we reach them better. This prevents bigger problems. Early help is cheaper and more humane.

115091.explanation: There are more electric cars. That means more charging spots are needed too. We choose locations in consultation with residents. This way we keep the street neat and usable.

115093.explanation: Good care is also appropriate care, but in these times we must be realistic and primarily consider the availability of care providers.

115094.explanation: Youth care is about quality and results. A low price without good help helps no one. So we look beyond just money. Prevention and cooperation count.

115095.explanation: We want to keep the total charges low. At the same time, the budget must be balanced. We do not make empty promises. We aim for stable and affordable charges where the increase in total tax burden stays below 5%.

115097.explanation: Companies provide jobs, including for our youth. We investigate where expansion is truly necessary. Sustainable and well-integrated is the standard. We prevent vacancy and aging.

115098.explanation: Our own residents have priority through the priority arrangement. We follow the imposed national standards for housing status holders.

115103.explanation: Swimming saves lives, especially in a water-rich region. School swimming helps every child become safe in the water. That is good and fair. We do expect parents to also contribute, so we share the costs together.

115107.explanation: We pay attention to the village character and the surroundings. Tall apartment blocks often do not fit there. We prefer to build in scale with the village. Beautiful, green, and livable.

115108.explanation: Room-by-room rental often puts pressure on the neighborhood. Think about parking and livability. We want peace in residential areas. We look for other solutions to the housing shortage.

115109.explanation: Accessibility is important for shops and hospitality. Enough parking spaces help with that. We also pay attention to safety and traffic flow. And to a neat, green appearance.

115110.explanation: A car-free center can be pleasant and beautiful. But accessibility and parking are also important. We will first investigate the effects. We choose what works best for everyone.

115111.explanation: Affordable construction is important to us. But a fixed 50% rule does not fit everywhere. We determine the best mix per location. This way we maintain pace and quality.

115112.explanation: We do not want reception locations because Halderberge already houses many status holders. That puts pressure on facilities and support. If we receive a national assignment for housing, we do this in good consultation with residents about locations and numbers.

115113.explanation: Bodycams help with safety and evidence. They protect enforcement officers and residents. Recordings provide clarity in incidents. We use them according to privacy regulations.

115061.explanation: WOS Halderberge considers careful and clear weighing of interests through participation very important. The referendum could be one of the tools. There are also other forms that may be better suited depending on the topic and its impact.

115063.explanation: WOS Halderberge supports the expansion of functions in agricultural areas. Of course, it is important to maintain a proper balance in the range of functions.

115068.explanation: WOS Halderberge finds good information important to be able to burn safely for yourself and the environment.

115070.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes that a fireworks show can be facilitated in each village.

115071.explanation: WOS Halderberge thinks that bulky waste collection is currently well arranged. Much can be brought free of charge to the recycling center, and for residual waste, the polluter pays.

115073.explanation: WOS Halderberge sees this as a form of responsible corporate social responsibility and therefore a personal responsibility.

115074.explanation: WOS Halderberge is certainly in favor of generous function broadening for existing and retiring farmers, but there should also remain space for enterprising farmers who should not be unnecessarily hindered by residential homes in rural areas. We also value our beautiful countryside.

115075.explanation: WOS Halderberge supports vitality and sports for everyone, including people with less financial means.

115076.explanation: WOS Halderberge considers the current contribution sufficient.

115080.explanation: WOS Halderberge supports more enforcement officers as long as this results in better visibility and a significant improvement in our feeling of safety.

115081.explanation: WOS Halderberge does not see this as a problem in hotspots or places where repeated nuisance has been observed.

115082.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes better communication should be focused on concerning possible reception options in case of an unwanted situation.

115085.explanation: WOS Halderberge does not see these homes as suitable for permanent residence, but under certain conditions, they could be used temporarily (a few months) for urgent seekers.

115086.explanation: WOS Halderberge sees opportunities to allow housing development on the edges of the villages.

115088.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes that residents with a low income should not be pushed further into trouble.

115089.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes that subsistence security should be the norm.

115091.explanation: WOS Halderberge feels the balance between electric and non-electric vehicles must remain correct, and there should also be a solution for home chargers without a private driveway.

115093.explanation: WOS Halderberge supports freedom of choice.

115094.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes the costs of youth care should be reduced and wants to focus on lower prices but certainly also on innovation and reform of the youth care system.

115095.explanation: For WOS Halderberge, the principle is that property tax (OZB) should be as low as possible.

115097.explanation: WOS Halderberge does see possibilities in optimizing and improving the quality of existing sites.

115098.explanation: WOS Halderberge sees possibilities for specific temporary transition locations for this target group.

115103.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes that financial support should be possible for low-income residents.

115107.explanation: WOS Halderberge supports this if it fits within the space and landscape.

115108.explanation: WOS Halderberge thinks this is a good idea provided that space is offered to local residents, young and old, at an affordable price.

115109.explanation: WOS Halderberge finds it important that parking can take place in the vicinity of the center. Unnecessarily many cars in the centers is not desired.

115110.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes that discouraging car traffic in the center is desirable (car-reduced).

115111.explanation: WOS Halderberge follows the current housing agreement arrangements.

115112.explanation: WOS Halderberge does not support a large-scale asylum seekers center. Humane, small-scale housing, preferably spread over the municipality, for people who are truly in need (not fortune seekers) can count on our support provided safety for people and the environment can be guaranteed.

115113.explanation: WOS Halderberge believes that our BOAs should be able to do their work with good equipment. In our opinion, this includes a bodycam and possibly more protective/supportive tools.

115061.explanation: We think there are better ways to involve residents in discussions, such as citizen assemblies and other local consultations with residents.

115063.explanation: In principle, we are in favor, but it must fit within the environment.

115070.explanation: The municipality must facilitate this. Residents can organize this themselves by founding a fireworks association or entrusting it to an Oranje committee or another club.

115071.explanation: The current arrangement is perfectly adequate.

115073.explanation: Given the aging population, it is important that buildings are accessible to everyone.

115074.explanation: We believe this should be organized within the Space for Space regulation that is currently being prepared.

115075.explanation: We do not agree with full payment. We do support sports with discounts or a reduced subscription fee for low-income groups.

115076.explanation: We value local and regional broadcasters as important for the community.

115080.explanation: We do not want more BOAs. We want the BOAs to work thematically. For example, focusing on themes like nuisance in rural areas or drug-related disorder.

115081.explanation: We believe this measure should be used in places where livability or safety is at risk.

115082.explanation: We are in principle not against a women's shelter, but we think this should be decided regionally.

115085.explanation: We are strongly opposed to permanent residence in holiday homes.

115086.explanation: We disagree. We actually advocate for adding a street in every village.

115088.explanation: We find the current arrangement good and do not want to tamper with it.

115089.explanation: We believe the income limit in Halderberge is set high enough. Therefore, we are not in favor of an increase.

115091.explanation: We believe that electric driving is the future. Therefore, more charging points must be made available.

115093.explanation: If people want this, they should pay for it themselves.

115094.explanation: We find quality just as important as price. The price/quality ratio should be leading, not who offers youth care the cheapest.

115095.explanation: Property tax can be lowered if economic circumstances permit.

115097.explanation: In principle, there are enough business parks. We should focus on revitalizing existing business parks where necessary.

115098.explanation: We cannot explain to our residents why they should wait for a rental home because others from outside receive priority. We acknowledge this causes problems with housing status holders. National politics will need to take a position on this.

115103.explanation: There are national provisions that cover these costs for children from low-income families.

115107.explanation: We think high residential flats do not fit in a rural municipality consisting of five villages. Residential flats belong in cities.

115108.explanation: We are not in favor of renting out by rooms.

115109.explanation: The current parking policy is sufficient. If there are insufficient parking spaces, the municipal council can adjust.

115110.explanation: We are not in favor of a car-free center in Oudenbosch. All residents must be able to reach their homes by car.

115111.explanation: We support more affordable rental and owner-occupied housing. More than 50% new construction is too much in our view.

115112.explanation: We must comply with the Distribution Act. If we have to choose, we prefer multiple small locations rather than one large asylum seekers center.

115113.explanation: This should be a free choice by the BOA themselves.

115061.explanation: D66 wants to actively involve residents in decision-making through referenda, citizen forums, and other forms of participation.

115063.explanation: Spatial planning requires customization. Not every location is suitable, and livability and nature must be protected.

115068.explanation: Wood burning worsens air quality. D66 chooses health and clean air and therefore participates in the Clean Air Agreement. With good information, use of the burning guide, and phasing out polluting stoves, wood burning can remain partially allowed.

115070.explanation: D66 supports a fireworks ban, and a fireworks show is a safe alternative causing less nuisance.

115071.explanation: D66 wants to promote waste separation but also prevent misuse and high costs.

115073.explanation: D66 wants to stimulate the accessibility of buildings, such as sports venues, events, and public buildings.

115074.explanation: We stand for preserving landscape and nature with space for farmers, but building cannot be allowed everywhere without restriction.

115075.explanation: Sports promote health and social participation. Money must not be a barrier.

115076.explanation: Local media are important for democracy and information provision. Budgets should be matched to population and viewer density. The regional broadcaster is currently somewhat invisible.

115080.explanation: Residents repeatedly say that 'the police feel distant.' More BOAs increase visibility and safety in neighborhoods and ensure that neighborhood officers can free up time for conversations, signaling, and community contacts.

115081.explanation: Sometimes camera surveillance is the only way to address unsafe situations. D66 supports camera surveillance where there are proven risks. However, we believe the use of camera surveillance should mostly be a temporary solution.

115082.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be safely accommodated. D66 believes the municipality has an important role in detecting, preventing, and tackling domestic violence by appointing a confidant in every community through the village center or neighborhood team.

115085.explanation: Holiday homes are not a structural solution to the housing shortage.

115086.explanation: D66 wants to tackle the housing crisis by building wherever possible, both inside and outside existing village boundaries. By making smart choices, we ensure our villages remain livable and future-proof.

115088.explanation: Debt counselors are given the space to offer low-threshold and proactive debt assistance based on their expertise. The municipality takes over problematic debts so people can start building a carefree and sustainable future without fear of bailiffs.

115089.explanation: More people need support due to rising costs.

115091.explanation: More and more residents in our municipality are driving electric vehicles, and this number is rapidly increasing. Charging close to home is not always easy. We want more charging stations, including in newly built neighborhoods in our municipality.

115093.explanation: D66 believes everyone should have the freedom to choose their own care services that suit them, as this increases quality and trust. However, this must be certified, high-quality care that fits within existing regulations.

115094.explanation: For D66, quality of care comes first, not the lowest price. High-quality youth care and limiting waiting lists prevent expensive care costs in the future.

115095.explanation: The municipality needs income to maintain facilities. The budget must be balanced, so we do not spend more than we receive. If there is an unexpected shortfall, we aim to restore budget balance as soon and responsibly as possible.

115097.explanation: D66 wants to redevelop existing business parks to counter vacancy and better align the supply with the demand of (local) entrepreneurs. The creation of new business parks will only be considered if there is no room left on existing sites.

115098.explanation: Quick housing promotes integration. The municipality has a national obligation to house status holders. D66 believes we must meet our legal obligations.

115103.explanation: School swimming increases safety and equal opportunities. Swimming lessons have become unaffordable for many parents, causing fewer children to learn to swim.

115107.explanation: D66 supports high-rise buildings, provided they fit the environment. We must use our space carefully, so this will not be possible in every village or at every location.

115108.explanation: D66 supports renting rooms under the 'hospitation' arrangement where the landlord also lives in the building. We oppose using rental homes for separate room rentals.

115109.explanation: D66 supports addressing traffic problems in Oudenbosch by, for example, creating extra parking spaces and introducing blue zones in and around the center. This should not come at the expense of livability.

115110.explanation: D66 favors making the center of Oudenbosch low-traffic, as this can contribute to livability and traffic safety.

115111.explanation: D66 strives for more affordable housing.

115112.explanation: Distribution ensures better integration, which we will do together in a way that fits within our villages. We also pay attention to the safety, health, and well-being of asylum seekers.

115113.explanation: Bodycams can help increase safety, especially when there is a concrete safety necessity. Bodycams should never replace adequate staffing, training, or community-oriented work. Privacy must also be carefully protected.

115061.explanation: A referendum can have value in certain forms (for example, among randomly selected residents) on specific issues. A referendum is not an end in itself, and we are not at all in favor of binding referendums. We prefer expanding speaking opportunities and citizen councils.

115063.explanation: Basically, we believe farmers should be supported in adapting their core activities. For instance, keeping fewer livestock may require financial compensation, which could be through allowing a shop or campsite. This will always be tailored and not automatic.

115068.explanation: In case of poor air quality (as is the case in Halderberge), it must be possible to prohibit activities, at least in areas where people live close together. However, we believe residents should be supported with subsidies or loans to switch to alternatives.

115070.explanation: To preserve the tradition, we advocate for firework shows organized by the municipality.

115071.explanation: For low-income people and recyclable items, yes.

115073.explanation: This is not only the responsibility of an entrepreneur but a collective societal responsibility, with the government stepping in financially to literally and figuratively lower barriers.

115074.explanation: We support farmers who want to use their existing farms for small-scale and sustainable housing, but we will always block large-scale commercial housing developments that sacrifice agricultural land en masse without a social component.

115075.explanation: Sports are good for health, social cohesion, and participation and must be accessible to everyone.

115076.explanation: Local journalism is the watchdog of local democracy and should be able to invest in professionalization with additional financial support.

115080.explanation: We prefer more capacity for the police and more community officers. They should have more (investigative) powers and training.

115081.explanation: Basically, cameras in certain places will displace problems rather than solve them. But in serious cases, they can be used as a last resort.

115082.explanation: We have arranged this regionally. Shelters for victims of domestic violence are usually not desirable within their own environment, so they are not necessarily needed in our municipality. But we would certainly support it if the region wanted to establish one in our municipality.

115085.explanation: At least as long as there is a housing shortage. This can be through legalization (under strict conditions) or a temporary (personal) tolerance permit. Safety and livability must be guaranteed.

115086.explanation: Where there is still space within built-up areas, we will oppose expansion outside the built-up area. When boundaries are truly reached, we will agree under strict conditions (such as mostly social and affordable housing, not commercial, small-scale, and with nature preservation).

115088.explanation: We must protect the lowest incomes (around the subsistence level such as social assistance or small pensions) against poverty and debts.

115089.explanation: There is too much hidden poverty among people who earn just above social assistance levels. We believe there are many reasons, but this alone justifies raising the income limit for receiving help.

115091.explanation: The actual need for charging points must be considered, not designated blindly. That is why we advocate a proactive vision so that we can cluster needs in strategic locations during new construction or redesign or directly include them in the infrastructure being built.

115093.explanation: We believe people do not have a compulsory right of choice, but care institutions should do their utmost to accommodate preferences where possible.

115094.explanation: We are strongly opposed to this because quality must always come before price. Therefore, we also want to end complicated and expensive tendering procedures that cause uncertainty and frequent changes of care providers every few years. So away with market forces.

115095.explanation: This almost always comes at the cost of local facilities—in other words, budget cuts. We prefer a stable property tax level so investments can be made in our villages.

115097.explanation: Our focus is not on quantity (more square meters) but on quality and reusing existing space. No more space for distribution centers but for local entrepreneurs and innovative, sustainable companies.

115098.explanation: For us, people who need priority due to divorce, fire, or other valid reasons, as well as people with a residence permit, are equally important. One is not less important than the other.

115103.explanation: With all the water in our municipality and country, we see swimming safety as a basic right. In our opinion, every child should have the chance to obtain a swimming diploma regardless of their parents' income.

115107.explanation: Not in favor because this does not fit the village character of our villages.

115108.explanation: This has resulted in massive housing of multiple labor migrants in regular homes, which are vacated every 3 to 6 months for new people. No social cohesion, nuisance, and pressure on the housing market for regular home seekers.

115109.explanation: More space for greenery, cyclists, and pedestrians. Of course, parking standards must still be met.

115110.explanation: Yes, but with some conditions: there must remain good facilities for people with disabilities and for loading and unloading for local shopkeepers. Tailored solutions must also be made for residents so they can (if necessary temporarily) access their homes.

115111.explanation: Certainly, with a focus on starters and seniors! For our young people taking their first step onto the housing market and our seniors wanting affordable single-floor homes. No luxury projects with houses most people cannot afford.

115112.explanation: We decisively choose multiple small-scale shelter locations rather than one large asylum seeker center. Small-scale locations have less impact on the environment, provide refugees with more privacy and safety, which will improve their well-being and speed up integration.

115113.explanation: Increases the safety of our enforcement officers (BOAs) and sends a preventive signal (people generally behave more calmly when being filmed). Conversely, it can also be used to objectively assess complaints against a BOA. However, strict rules about the recordings are necessary, including retention time and privacy.

statements.title: All municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Halderberge must wear a body camera.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa) in de gemeente Halderberge moeten een bodycam dragen

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of Halderberge must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Halderberge moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Halderberge must accommodate asylum seekers at multiple small locations.

titleParts.text: Halderberge moet op meerdere kleine locaties

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: In Halderberge the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Halderberge moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety must be deployed in Halderberge.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: A women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) must be established in Halderberge.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
More camera surveillance must be introduced in Halderberge.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More business parks must be developed in Halderberge.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Halderberge.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The center of Oudenbosch must become car-free.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
At least 50% of new housing in the municipality must be affordable owner-occupied or rental housing.
